metadata.description: Anterior leg compartment containing dorsiflexor musculature.
Lateral leg bone influencing alignment and length in tibial shaft injuries.
Four-compartment osteofascial model of the lower leg.
Segmented diaphyseal pattern suggesting instability and operative tendency.
Endosteal and periosteal perfusion concepts relevant to healing and open fractures.
Composite leg model linking tibial shaft, fibula, blood supply, and compartment layout.
Intramedullary nailing for unstable or operative tibial shaft fracture patterns.
Deep infection risk especially with open tibial shaft fractures.
Angular or rotational malalignment after tibial shaft fracture.
Failure of fracture healing after tibial shaft injury.
Subcutaneous tibial diaphysis with limited soft-tissue envelope.
High-risk antecedent injury frequently associated with leg compartment syndrome.
